channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Democrats fight for Tester in Montana as Sheehy pulls away
item.description: Democrats are refusing to give up on Sen. Jon Tester’s (D-Mont.) reelection bid even as his chances appear bleak with just more than two weeks until Election Day.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, NewsNation

item.title: Montana GOP candidate who could flip control of Senate nagged by claims he lied about bullet wound
item.description: BILLINGS, Mont. (AP) — A former Park Service ranger said Friday that U.S. Senate hopeful Tim Sheehy of Montana has been lying about a bullet wound that the candidate said came from fighting in Afghanistan — going public with an accusation that has nagged the Republican’s campaign for months.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW BROWN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The US is investigating an unauthorized release of classified documents on Israel's attack plans
item.description: The United States is investigating an unauthorized release of classified documents that assess Israel’s plans to attack Iran and note that Israel continues to move military assets in place to conduct a military strike in response to Iran’s blistering ballistic missile attack on Oct. 1.
item.dc:creator: Zeke Miller, Mike Balsalmo, Eric Tucker And Tara Copp, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Harris says Trump is 'cruel' as she spotlights abortion restrictions in Georgia during early voting
item.description: Vice President Kamala Harris says Republican former President Donald Trump was “cruel” for how he talked about the grieving family of a Georgia mother who died after waiting 20 hours for a hospital to treat her complications from an abortion pill.
item.dc:creator: Chris Megerian, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Minimum wage on the ballot in these 5 states
item.description: The federal minimum wage hasn't changed since 2009 but voters in several states will have the chance to raise hourly wages at the state level.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Dorn, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Harris eyes a rural Maine congressional district in a hunt for every possible electoral vote
item.description: LEWISTON, Maine (AP) — Rob Rogers has seen his rural corner of New England turn into a stronghold for Donald Trump in two consecutive elections. But this year he's feeling hopeful that Vice President Kamala Harris could reclaim a potentially decisive electoral vote for Democrats.
item.dc:creator: PATRICK WHITTLE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The Latest: Trump and Harris are campaigning for votes in pivotal Michigan
item.description: With the Nov. 5 election fast approaching, Democrat Kamala Harris and Republican Donald Trump are setting their sights on the key battleground state of Michigan on Friday. The vice president began her day in Grand Rapids before holding events in Lansing and Oakland County, northwest of Detroit.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Big bets on Trump don’t mean the market is rigged: Betting analyst
item.description: Michael Schwimer says a flood of bets for Trump can’t rig the markets any more than a big bet on the World Cup will affect how the game is played.
item.dc:creator: Rich Johnson, NewsNation

item.title: Biden's gone silent on Ukraine support, ranking member of Armed Services Committee warns
item.description: Roger Wicker, the head of the Senate’s armed forces committee, urged President Biden to aggressively accelerate aid to Ukraine in his final weeks in office.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Senate Republicans raise questions after funds for disaster loans exhausted
item.description: A group of GOP senators are pressing the Small Business Administration (SBA) for answers after officials said its disaster loan program exhausted its funds earlier this week in the aftermath of a pair of major hurricanes.
item.dc:creator: Aris Folley, NewsNation

item.title: FLASHBACK: Harris declined to intervene on behalf of native tribe push to protect sacred site from wind farm
item.description: CA AG Kamala Harris' office said it did not have legal standing to take up the case of a Native American group fighting to preserve sacred burial ground from a wind farm.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: What's a 'Jezebel spirit'? Some Christians use the term to paint Kamala Harris with a demonic brush
item.description: Christian nationalist leaders are telling followers that Vice President Kamala Harris is under the influence of a “Jezebel spirit,” using a term with deeply racist and misogynistic roots that is setting off alarm bells for religious and political scholars.
item.dc:creator: ISABELLA VOLMERT and MICHELLE R. SMITH, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Barbed wire, high fences: some election spots in Arizona close, but others step up
item.description: Some election locations in Arizona are shutting their doors in the face of security threats — but others are expanding their footprint.
item.dc:creator: Ximena Bustillo, MPR News

item.title: Following death of Hamas leader, Harris says it's 'time for the day after to begin' in Gaza
item.description: MILWAUKEE (AP) — Kamala Harris said Thursday that Israel's killing of Hamas' top leader offers "an opportunity to finally end the war in Gaza," talking plainly about next steps even as the pro-Palestinian demonstrators who gathered outside her campaign event underscored the complicated politics at p
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN, COLLEEN LONG and STEVE KARNOWSKI, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: 3 states renew efforts to limit abortion pill access
item.description: The lawsuit was filed in a Texas federal court Friday, roughly four months after the Supreme Court decided against undoing changes to the FDA’s regulations on the abortion pill that expanded access — such as allowing it to be mailed without an in-person doctor’s visit.
item.dc:creator: Alejandra O'Connell-Domenech, Fox 5

item.title: Nebraska court allows voter registrations from people with felony convictions
item.description: Nebraskans with felony convictions may register to vote after the state's highest court ruled Wednesday that a top election official had no authority to deem a law restoring those rights unconstitutional.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, Fox 5

item.title: Eric Adams welcomes new administration members amid wave of resignations
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – Some new faces are joining Mayor Eric Adams' administration as it works to stabilize City Hall after a series of high-level exits. A new city schools chancellor and a new deputy mayor for public safety are taking the reins.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, PIX 11

item.title: Harris is pressed on the concerns of Black voters in Charlamagne tha God town hall
item.description: Harris’ town hall with the influential radio host came at a crucial moment in the campaign, as polls show her struggling to cement the level of support she may need from Black men to win the White House.
item.dc:creator: Deepa Shivaram and Jason Breslow, MPR News

item.title: Election Day Looms: Hurricane impacts on voting
item.description: Voting rules changed for impacted Florida counties after hurricane Helene, now the question is, will rules be adjusted after hurricane Milton?
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Shannon Bilbray-Axelrod discusses growth, traffic, and affordable housing in county commissioner race
item.description: Shannon Bilbray-Axelrod, a candidate for county commissioner and current Nevada Assembly member, discussed her key priorities for her district on the 8 News Now Daily Voting Guide.
item.dc:creator: Justin Walker, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Eric Adams is 'working well through the chaos': Gov. Hochul
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – Gov. Kathy Hochul offered support for embattled New York City Mayor Eric Adams during the Columbus Day Parade in Manhattan on Monday. "Told him to bring in new blood… he's doing that… working well through the chaos," she told PIX11 News.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, PIX 11
